meta.description: Design a structured case-control study framework with explicit source population logic, control selection rules, matching decisions, exposure measurement planning, and bias-control checkpoints.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or unapproved treatment recommendations produced.

methodological_ground.detail: Strong source-population discipline and matching overmatching warnings enforced

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 2, design planning only

functional_suitability.note: Description is very brief (one sentence); does not adequately communicate the full scope and trigger contexts of the skill

reliability.note: Proceeds with conditional language when inputs are unclear; no active minimum-clarification protocol defined

human_usability.note: Very brief description reduces discoverability; users may not recognize this skill without knowing epidemiology terminology

security.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision low due to brief description; no dedicated progressive disclosure of reference modules in description

inputs.label: Case-control study for postoperative pulmonary complications after major abdominal surgery

assertions.text: Case-control design fit explicitly assessed before proceeding with design
Source population defined such that both cases and controls arise from same population
Matching strategy decision explicitly justified or rejected with overmatching risk noted
Exposure timing and recall bias risks explicitly addressed
No fabricated event rates, registry details, or guideline endorsements

inputs.label: Hospital-based case-control study on antibiotic exposure and Clostridioides difficile infection

assertions.text: Incident vs prevalent case distinction addressed
Exposure window defined with temporal alignment to outcome
Bias-control matrix present with at least selection, recall, information, and confounding rows
Odds ratio vs risk ratio distinction addressed
No clinical utility claims made beyond study scope

inputs.label: Community-based matched case-control study on occupational exposure and rare lymphoma

assertions.text: Rare outcome justification for case-control stated
Matching strategy assessed with analytic consequences stated
Conditional logistic regression recommended when individual matching is proposed
Recall bias for occupational exposure explicitly flagged
No post-outcome measurement accepted as valid baseline exposure

inputs.label: Biomarker measured after diagnosis — validity of using as etiologic exposure

assertions.text: Post-diagnosis biomarker correctly flagged as invalid etiologic exposure without qualification
Reverse-timing distortion risk explicitly named
Alternative study framing recommended when biomarker timing is post-outcome
No case-control design rejected outright without offering viable alternative
Primary recommendation given even when design fit is weak
assertions.note: Output correctly identified design unsuitability but did not provide a clean single recommended alternative design configuration

inputs.label: Complex pharmacoepidemiology case-control with multiple exposure windows and confounders by indication

assertions.text: Confounding by indication explicitly identified as major bias risk
Multiple exposure windows handled with explicit timeline structure
Primary statistical analysis line stated (conditional logistic or logistic regression)
Feasibility and interpretation limits separated from design recommendations
No fabricated drug prevalence, event rates, or guideline positions

key_strengths: Strong source-population and sampling-frame discipline enforced throughout — prevents fundamental case-control design errors
Matching hard rules are excellent: no default matching, overmatching risk explicitly flagged, analytic consequences always stated
Five reference modules cover question-fit, case/control definition, matching/exposure, bias/analysis, and output style comprehensively
Explicit prohibition on using post-outcome biomarkers as etiologic exposures — a common error in clinical research

recommendations.title: Description is too brief to provide adequate triggering signal
recommendations.problem: The description is a single short sentence that names only the output type, not the user scenarios or trigger contexts. Users seeking help with retrospective study design, pharmacoepidemiology, or rare disease exposure studies may not trigger this skill.
recommendations.root_cause: Description was not optimized for natural-language triggering; no trigger scenarios, user phrases, or scope qualifiers included.
recommendations.fix: Expand description to include trigger phrases such as 'retrospective case-control', 'rare outcome design', 'pharmacoepidemiology bias control', 'matching strategy', and 'hospital-based controls' to improve triggering recall.

recommendations.title: Output Section L (Primary Recommendation) missing when design fit is weak
recommendations.problem: When the case-control design is found to be poorly aligned with the research question, the output identified the problem but failed to produce a clean single recommended alternative, leaving the user without actionable guidance.
recommendations.root_cause: The mandatory output structure requires a primary recommendation but does not provide an explicit redirection protocol when the case-control design is rejected.
recommendations.fix: Add an explicit redirection rule to Section L: when case-control design is rated 'poorly aligned', output a specific 1–2 sentence recommended alternative design (e.g., 'a prospective cohort would better address this prognostic question') and the main reason.

recommendations.title: No minimum clarification protocol before full design output
recommendations.problem: The Input Validation section instructs cautious inference when inputs are missing, but does not define when to ask follow-up questions vs proceed with assumptions.
recommendations.root_cause: No explicit minimum-clarification check is defined.
recommendations.fix: Add 2–4 high-yield clarifying questions to ask when outcome type or exposure timing is unclear, as these are the most common causes of design errors in case-control studies.
